title: Today's Tech Brief: That an app 'Fits on a Floppy' is still a useful measure in 2026
summary: A look at the relevance of floppy disk space in modern app development, and other tech news.

body.text: The article suggests that in a world where bot-laden software is the norm, small, efficient apps are more beautiful than ever. This speaks to a larger trend in the tech industry - the economics of cloud computing have led to a proliferation of bloated, resource-intensive applications. But there's a growing recognition that smaller, more streamlined software can be more secure, more efficient, and more user-friendly. It's almost like the tech industry is rediscovering the joys of minimalism.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
So that is the brief version: a few headlines, a little context, and just enough connective tissue to make the day easier to follow without turning the whole thing into homework.

sources.title: That an app 'Fits on a Floppy' is still a useful measure in 2026
